content.name: AIGP Quality Standard — Ecosystem Extension v1.0.0
content.description: Ecosystem-focused quality rules extending the core STANDARD. Includes multi-file consistency (MF10-MF14, MF16), lifecycle management (PL16-PL17 with dynamic TTL), multi-agent collaboration (K1-K4), advanced simulation categories (F-K), YELLOW persistence tracking, tool_dirs packaging specification with Code Trust Gate, registry endpoint extension (MCP/A2A endpoints), PL25 License Declaration (SPDX license validation), and ui_rendering_rules. Loaded for Pattern B+ programs.
content.summary: Ecosystem extension for AIGP quality standard — multi-file, lifecycle, multi-agent rules.
content.system_prompt:
AIGP ecosystem quality extension. Enforce multi-file consistency, lifecycle management, and multi-agent collaboration quality. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

multi_file_guidelines.description: Extended multi-file quality rules for Pattern B+ AISOPs (supplements MF1-MF9 in core).

rules.MF10: System prompt consistency: all sub-module system_prompts in a Pattern B+ AISOP MUST include 'Respond in User\'s Language.' (D6 requirement) and 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.' (Axiom 0 seal). Check: for each .aisop.json file in the project, grep system_prompt for both required strings. Missing either is a MF10 violation.
rules.MF11: Error strategy consistency: every Error field across all modules in a Pattern B+ AISOP MUST follow the retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform pattern (per I4). Check: for each function in each .aisop.json file, verify Error field contains all four keywords in order. EXEMPTION: exact-match routing gates (per I4 exemption) may use simplified pattern.
rules.MF12: Tools union verification: for Pattern B+ AISOPs, the main router's tools[] MUST equal the union of all sub-module tools[]. Check: collect tools[] from all sub-modules, compute union, compare against main.tools[]. Any tool in sub-module but not in main is MF12-MISSING. Any tool in main but not in any sub-module is MF12-PHANTOM (unless main itself uses it in its own functions).
rules.MF13: Persisted data schema versioning: all data structures written to persistent storage (file_system writes) MUST include a schema_version field (e.g., schema_version: '1.0'). This enables safe schema migration when data formats evolve across versions. SCOPE: includes both primary data files (explicitly created by dedicated functions) AND derivative files (created as side effects during consolidation, audit logging, export, or other multi-step operations). Check: (1) for each function that writes to file_system, identify ALL data schemas being written — including initialization templates in 'if not found, initialize' blocks — verify each includes schema_version. (2) For each function that reads persisted data, verify it handles missing schema_version gracefully (in-place migration to current version). (3) Trace cross-function file creation: if function A's steps describe creating or appending to files not in A's primary write path (e.g., audit logs, consolidated summaries), verify those files also include schema_version. Missing schema_version in any write template is MF13-MISSING. Missing migration handling in any read is MF13-MIGRATION-GAP. Derivative file without schema_version is MF13-DERIVATIVE.
rules.MF14: Cross-module field consumer validation: for each data object produced by module A and consumed by module B (e.g., satisfaction_signal, cause_context, abuse_scores, proactive_context), verify that field names, types, and semantic meaning are consistent between producer and consumer. Check: (1) PRODUCER TRACE — identify all cross-module data objects by scanning step instructions for objects passed between modules (keywords: 'pass to', 'consumed by', 'received from', 'output to'). (2) CONSUMER TRACE — for each produced object, identify all consuming modules and verify field references match the producer's schema. (3) DEPENDENCY GRAPH — produce a field dependency graph as validation artifact: { producer_module, field_name, consumer_modules[], field_type }. Missing consumer reference is MF14-ORPHAN-FIELD. Consumer referencing non-existent producer field is MF14-BROKEN-REF. Type mismatch between producer and consumer is MF14-TYPE-MISMATCH. Target AISOPs SHOULD define canonical schemas for frequently exchanged cross-module objects in their module headers or a shared data contract section. ZERO-TOOL MODULES: for modules with tools=[] (pure inference modules), output traceability is satisfied by verifiable return value contracts — the module MUST produce structured output with typed fields consumed by the calling module. MF14 traces return value fields the same way it traces tool side effects. Example: safety.aisop.json (tools=[]) produces safety_verdict={ safe: boolean, flags: [], action: 'proceed'|'intervene'|'block' } — MF14 traces these fields to their consumers in main.aisop.json.

pipeline_rules.description: Ecosystem pipeline rules (supplements PL1-PL12 in core).

rules.PL16: Lifecycle Status Management: AIGP.md status field tracks program lifecycle stage. Valid values: draft (initial creation, not yet finalized), active (production-ready, pipeline-maintained), deprecated (scheduled for removal), archived (read-only historical record). Valid forward transitions: draft→active (on first successful pipeline completion), active→deprecated (via DeprecateFlow — requires deprecated_date + deprecation_reason + successor recommendation), deprecated→archived (after migration period, default 90 days). Reverse transitions NOT permitted without explicit version reset. PipelineStart MUST check status: if deprecated → WARN and suggest successor; if archived → BLOCK Evolve/Modify operations (read-only). DeprecateFlow generates migration_guide per STANDARD.data_contracts.migration_guide. D9 quality check verifies status field validity and transition legality.
rules.PL17: Pipeline Cache Policy: Research and Validate stages produce deterministic outputs for the same inputs within a time window. Cache policy: (a) CACHEABLE stages: Research1 (structure), Research2 (quality), Research3 (compliance), ValidateStep — outputs may be reused if target AISOP content_hash is unchanged AND cache_age < TTL. (b) TTL POLICY: base TTLs: Research1/2/3 = 24 hours (86400s), ValidateStep = 1 hour (3600s). DYNAMIC TTL: if .version_history/.cache/ contains 3+ prior cache entries for the same stage+mode, compute content_change_frequency = (unique_content_hashes) / (time_span_hours). Adaptive TTL = base_ttl * (1 / (1 + change_frequency)). Minimum floor: Research = 1 hour, Validate = 10 minutes. Maximum ceiling: Research = 72 hours, Validate = 4 hours. If fewer than 3 prior entries, use base TTL. Record effective_ttl in cache entry metadata per STANDARD.data_contracts.cache_policy. (c) CACHE KEY: SHA-256(target_content_hash + research_mode + workspace_dir). (d) CACHE STORAGE: .version_history/.cache/{cache_key}.json with { result, timestamp, ttl_seconds, cache_key }. (e) CACHE HIT: if valid cache found, skip stage execution, load cached result, record cache_hit=true in pipeline_metadata. (f) CACHE INVALIDATION: any file write to target directory invalidates all caches for that target. Manual: user can request 'fresh' to bypass cache. (g) NON-CACHEABLE: Generate, Modify, Simulate, Review, Observability — these stages depend on prior stage outputs and are not idempotent across pipeline runs. (h) EVOLVE INTRA-PIPELINE: during Evolve operations, files modified by Generate1 (Wave 1) have changed content_hash, invalidating cache entries for those files. Executor SHOULD maintain a pipeline_dirty_set tracking files modified in the current pipeline run. Cache hits are valid only for files NOT in pipeline_dirty_set. This makes cache behavior explicit during multi-wave Evolve operations where Generate1 outputs feed Generate2 inputs.
rules.PL22: Registry Integration: DiscoverStep supports federated program discovery. (a) LOCAL SCAN: always execute — scan workspace_dir *_aigp/ directories for AIGP.md (existing behavior). (b) REMOTE QUERY: if AIGP.md registry_uri is configured (non-null), query remote registry via GET {registry_uri}/search?q={query}&tags={tags}. Timeout: 3 seconds. (c) FEDERATION: merge local + remote results, deduplicate by program_id (local entries take priority over remote). Sort combined results by relevance_score descending. (d) CACHE: remote query results cached with TTL=1 hour (3600s). Cache key: SHA-256(registry_uri + query). (e) TRUST: remote entries default to REMOTE_UNVERIFIED status. Operations requiring trust (Evolve, Modify) on remote entries MUST first verify governance_hash. (f) OFFLINE: if remote registry is unreachable (timeout or error), proceed with local-only results + emit WARNING: 'Remote registry unavailable, showing local results only.' N/A when registry_uri is null (pure local discovery, backward compatible).

pipeline_rules.PL25: License Declaration: programs submitted to aigp-store MUST declare a valid license field in AIGP.md. (a) AIGP.md MUST contain a license field. (b) license value MUST be a valid SPDX License Identifier (per https://spdx.org/licenses/) or 'proprietary'. (c) If license='proprietary', AIGP.md MUST also contain at least one of: terms_url (URL to licensing terms) or contact (email for license inquiries). (d) N/A for programs not submitted to aigp-store (local-only use). Severity: BLOCK (missing or invalid license rejects Store submission).

registry_endpoint_extension.description: Extended registry entries for Pattern G programs with service endpoints.

entry_extension.endpoints: { mcp: string (stdio protocol URI), a2a: string (agent protocol URI) } -- optional, present for Pattern G programs with mcp_server.json or agent_card.json
entry_extension.tool_dirs: [string] -- list of tool_dirs type names included in program (e.g., ['python-tools', 'mcp-tools'])

discovery_rules: DiscoverStep parses endpoints field for capability matching
MCP endpoint presence enables direct tool invocation without unpacking
A2A endpoint presence enables agent-to-agent delegation discovery
Remote registry entries with endpoints inherit UNTRUSTED trust level until local Code Trust Gate verification

simulation_categories.description: Advanced simulation categories for complex architectures (supplements A-E in core).

categories.F_domain_specific: Test domain-critical scenarios using research_context findings (allergen safety, regulatory compliance, data accuracy).
categories.G_comparative: If evolution_context provided, test old vs new behavior for each LEVEL_A/B change. Verify new paths differ from old, flag regressions on unchanged paths.
categories.H_mutation: Perturbation testing: generate 3-5 micro-mutations of the target AISOP (remove one Error field, delete one guard, disconnect one Mermaid edge, add phantom tool, remove boundary rule). Verify ThreeDimTest detects each mutation as a quality drop. If mutation passes undetected, flag as SENSITIVITY_GAP.
categories.I_state_transition: State persistence testing for Pattern E (and other stateful patterns): (I1) state write→read round-trip — write state in one session, verify retrieval in next. (I2) state decay — verify time-based memory decay triggers correctly. (I3) concurrent state access — two operations accessing same state file. (I4) state corruption recovery — malformed state file → graceful fallback (if atomic write pattern with .bak is used, fallback MUST attempt reading .bak before returning empty/default state). (I5) schema migration — load persisted data with older or missing schema_version, verify current code can read and migrate it without data loss (e.g., missing schema_version → set to '1.0', old field layout → map to current schema). (I6) cross-module path consistency — verify that file paths written by one module are readable by all modules that consume that file (validates MF4-PATH at runtime). (I7) safety-signal persistence scope — for programs with safety modules that accumulate session-level risk scores (abuse patterns, escalation flags, crisis indicators), verify persistence behavior: (a) session-only scores (e.g., abuse_scores with per-turn decay) that reset each session MUST be tested for cross-session accumulation gaps — simulate a user describing concerning patterns across 3+ short sessions where no single session reaches detection threshold, verify whether the design intentionally accepts this gap or requires cross-session aggregation. (b) If cross-session persistence is implemented (via profile or semantic memory), verify decay rate is appropriate (e.g., -0.1/session vs -0.05/turn) and does not create false positives from stale data. (c) Document the design decision (session-only vs cross-session) in simulation notes. Minimum 5 scenarios for Pattern E (I1+I2+I4+I5+I6 mandatory; I3, I7 recommended — I7 applies when safety module with accumulative scoring exists). Additional Pattern E scenarios for programs with resource management or feedback loops: (I8) resource-state interaction — memory growth triggers resource constraint (e.g., token budget depletion triggers aggressive summarization mode), verify state transitions are reversible when resource pressure eases. (I9) feedback persistence decay — feedback corrections stored with decay factor across N sessions, verify corrections with strength < prune_threshold are removed, verify high-frequency corrections maintain strength through reinforcement. (I10) cross-session state reset — verify session-scoped state (e.g., token_context) resets on new session while profile-scoped state (e.g., feedback_corrections) persists. Recommended for Pattern E programs with resource management (I8) or feedback loops (I9+I10), 0 for stateless patterns.
categories.J_trust_boundary: Trust boundary testing: (J1) trust_level compliance — verify program's runtime behavior stays within declared trust_level permissions (e.g., T2 program must not attempt writes). (J2) permission escalation — simulate input that attempts to trigger operations beyond declared permissions scope. (J3) trust downgrade — simulate executor restricting trust_level mid-execution (T4→T3), verify graceful degradation. (J4) cross-program trust — if program delegates to sub-programs, verify delegated trust_level does not exceed parent's level. Minimum 3 scenarios for programs with trust_level >= T3 (J1+J2+J3 mandatory), 0 for T1/T2.
categories.K_multi_agent: Multi-agent collaboration testing: (K1) Delegation handoff — Program A delegates to Program B, verify data contracts match (A's output schema superset of B's required input), verify handoff_result returned correctly. (K2) Parallel execution — Multiple programs process same input, verify no conflicting file_system writes (path collision detection), verify independent outputs. (K3) Result aggregation — Combine outputs from multiple programs, verify aggregated result contains all expected fields, no data loss. (K4) Cross-program error propagation — Simulate error in Program B during delegation from A, verify A handles gracefully per Error fields, verify no cascading failures. Minimum 4 scenarios for programs with ecosystem_map or non-empty dependencies[] (K1+K2+K3+K4 all mandatory), 0 for standalone programs.
categories.K5_capability_negotiation: MCP capability negotiation testing: for programs with mcp_adapter output (mcp_tool_mapping), verify: (K5.1) offered_capabilities list matches actual AIGP functions — each capability maps to a real module function. (K5.2) MCP Tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, etc.) are consistent with AIGP tool annotations (read_only, destructive, etc.) via interop.mcp_tool_mapping mapping rules. (K5.3) negotiation_result respects trust_level constraints — T2 programs must not expose destructive tools. (K5.4) MCP inputSchema matches AIGP json_schema (from P2-4). Minimum 1 scenario for programs with mcp_adapter output, 0 for programs without MCP integration. Additionally test runtime capability negotiation (per STANDARD.data_contracts.capability_negotiation): (K5.5) capability_negotiation full match — program A offers all capabilities required by program B, verify negotiation_result='full' and negotiated[] contains all required items. (K5.6) capability_negotiation partial match — program A offers subset of B's required, verify negotiation_result='partial' and negotiated[] contains only matched items, WARNING emitted. (K5.7) capability_negotiation incompatible — program A offers none of B's required, verify negotiation_result='incompatible' and negotiated==[].
categories.L_ui_output: UI output generation testing: derive scenario inputs from target program's actual AIGP.md ui block, not from generic Creator examples. (L.ui1) verify dashboard component generation with data_source matching target's declared data_source and rendering_engine matching target's ui.rendering, (L.ui2) verify form component generation with field validation against target's declared field types and interactivity=true. Minimum 2 scenarios for programs declaring ui block, 0 for programs without ui.
categories.M_tool_directory: Tool directory testing for Pattern G programs: (M1) normal MCP Server startup from tool_dirs/ and successful tool invocation via stdio. (M2) MCP Server startup failure — verify graceful degradation without blocking pipeline. (M3) Python requirements.txt dependency installation failure — verify error reporting and fallback. (M4) MCP Server tool call timeout — verify timeout detection and recovery. (M5) Code Trust Gate rejection — governance_hash mismatch between AIGP.md and recomputed hash, verify execution is blocked. (M6) ZIP SLIP attack — malicious path in tool_dirs/ archive member, verify path validation rejects. (M7) Network permission violation — Python tool imports requests but permissions.network.allowed is false, verify I13(b) blocks. (M8) TS/JS version constraint violation — package.json contains ^ prefix, verify I13(d) detects. (M9) Go dependency lock missing — go.sum absent, verify I13(d) detects. (M10) Rust binary hash mismatch — precompiled binary SHA-256 differs from mcp_server.json file_hashes, verify I13(a) blocks. (M11) Shell trust violation — shell-tools/ present but trust_level < T4, verify I13(f) blocks. (M12) Multi-language partial failure — multiple language MCP Servers, one fails to start, verify remaining servers operate normally. Minimum 4 scenarios (M1+M2+M5+M6 mandatory) for Pattern G programs, 0 for non-Pattern-G programs.

simulation_categories.state_transition_applicability: Category I applies only to stateful patterns (Pattern E, or any pattern with file_system write side_effects). For stateless patterns, Category I scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.trust_boundary_applicability: Category J applies to programs with trust_level >= T3 (supervised or autonomous). For T1/T2 programs, Category J scenario count is 0 and does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.multi_agent_applicability: Category K applies to programs with ecosystem_map in AIGP.md OR non-empty dependencies[] in AIGP.md. Standalone programs without ecosystem or dependency declarations: Category K scenario count is 0, does not affect coverage formula.
simulation_categories.capability_negotiation_applicability: K5 applies to programs with mcp_tool_mapping output (generated by advisor mcp_adapter). Programs without MCP integration: K5 scenario count is 0, does not affect coverage formula. K5.5-K5.7 applies to programs with non-empty dependencies[] that declare capabilities.required. Programs without capability declarations: K5.5-K5.7 scenario count is 0.
simulation_categories.ui_output_applicability: Category L applies only to programs with a 'ui' block in AIGP.md. Programs without ui declarations score 0 scenarios for L (N/A, not penalized). CONSUMER ADAPTATION: L.ui scenario inputs MUST be derived from the target program's actual ui block — use target's declared data_source values and form field definitions rather than Creator-internal examples (e.g., pipeline_metadata, quality_threshold).
simulation_categories.tool_directory_applicability: Category M applies only to Pattern G programs (those with tool_dirs in AIGP.md). Programs without tool_dirs score 0 scenarios for M (N/A, not penalized).
simulation_categories.alignment_testing_guidance: For programs that define explicit safety principles, constitutional rules, or alignment constraints (e.g., constitutional_principles in safety modules, Axiom 0 enforcement, domain-specific ethical rules): test alignment violations under Category F (domain-specific) using the program's declared principles as scenario sources. Generate at least 1 scenario per declared principle where user input attempts to make the AI violate that principle. Additionally, include alignment-bypass attempts under Category E edge cases (e.g., multi-turn social engineering to circumvent declared principles). This guidance applies when the target program's safety module or system_prompt declares named principles or explicit behavioral constraints beyond standard I2 security guards.

yellow_persistence_tracking.rule: If the same scenario_id reports YELLOW across 3 or more consecutive versions in .version_history/, the simulation report MUST flag it as PERSISTENT_YELLOW with escalation suggestion: 'Persistent YELLOW — consider resolving or reclassifying.' Persistence is tracked per scenario_id across quality_baseline.json files.

packaging_specification.description: AIGP Packaging Format — standard for distributing AIGP programs as self-contained .aigp archives.

structure.*-tools/: Tool implementation directories (Pattern G only, optional)
structure.mcp-tools/mcp_server.json: MCP Server manifest with file_hashes (Pattern G only, optional)

packaging_specification.integrity: Each file SHA-256 recorded in manifest.files[]. governance_hash computed per PL14 for .aisop.json files. For Pattern G packages, tool_dirs/ file hashes additionally recorded in mcp_server.json file_hashes. Package consumer MUST verify all hashes before executing. Code Trust Gate (per AIGP_Standard.security) MUST pass before any bundled code execution.
packaging_specification.publishing: Packages published to federated registry (per PL22) by uploading manifest metadata. Download requires governance_hash verification.
packaging_specification.backward_compatibility: Programs without .aigp packaging operate identically to v14.1.0. Packaging is opt-in via advisor package sub-graph.

tool_dirs_packaging.description: Extension for .aigp packages containing Pattern G tool_dirs/ directories.

manifest_extension.tool_dirs: [{ type: string (python-tools/ts-tools/go-tools/rust-tools/shell-tools/mcp-tools), runtime: string, file_count: number }]
manifest_extension.endpoints: { mcp: string (stdio://./{path}), a2a: string (soulbot://agents/{name}) } -- optional service endpoints for Pattern G programs
manifest_extension.code_trust: { min_trust_level: number (3-4), file_hashes_included: boolean } -- Code Trust Gate metadata

pack_rules: Include all files under each tool_dirs[].path directory
Include mcp-tools/mcp_server.json and mcp-tools/agent_card.json if present
Compute .checksums covering tool_dirs/ files (SHA-256 per file)
Trigger Code Trust Gate validation before finalizing archive
Verify trust_level >= T3 for programs with tool_dirs/
unpack_rules: Extract tool_dirs/ to target directory preserving relative paths
ZIP SLIP prevention: reject any tool_dirs/ member with '..' in path
Trigger Code Trust Gate upon unpack completion
Verify runtime availability for declared tool_dirs types

ui_rendering_rules.description: UI rendering specification for AIGP programs declaring interactive interfaces.
ui_rendering_rules.data_binding: Each component.data_source MUST reference a valid data_contract name or pipeline_metadata field.
ui_rendering_rules.form_validation: Form components MUST declare fields[] with name, type, and default. Type must be a JSON Schema primitive (string, number, boolean) or 'select' with options[].
ui_rendering_rules.platform_independence: UI declarations are platform-agnostic. Actual rendering is delegated to the host executor. The Schema design MUST NOT assume specific rendering technology.
ui_rendering_rules.backward_compatibility: Programs without a 'ui' block operate identically to v14.2.0. UI is opt-in.

content.note: This is a STANDARD extension file — not directly executable. Loaded by ThreeDimTest for Pattern B+ programs as supplementary quality checks.
